title: 10 best herbal essential oils for red eyes
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for red eyes are plant-derived extracts used to alleviate and soothe red, irritated eyes. They help by reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal essential oils for red eyes include Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Euphrasia officinalis, Rosmarinus officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Lavandula angustifolia, Melaleuca alternifolia, Hypericum perforatum, Eucalyptus globulus, and Cinchona officinalis. If left untreated, red eyes can lead to vision problems, headaches, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting daily life, work, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with red eyes because it has natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm irritated eyes. The oil's flavonoids and carotenoids work to reduce swelling and inflammation, while its antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial or viral infections that may be causing the redness. Additionally, pot marigold's gentle, non-irritating nature makes it a safe and effective solution for soothing dry, itchy, or bloodshot eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes caused by allergies, irritants, or other conditions.

Antioxidant: Calendula essential oil is rich in antioxidants, which can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to eye fatigue, dryness, and irritation, promoting healthy vision and overall eye well-being.

Wound-healing: Calendula essential oil has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and tissue repair, which can be beneficial for soothing and calming irritated or inflamed eyes, as well as reducing the appearance of dark circles or puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing eye irritation and swelling.
Seeds: Containing carotenoids and flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh calendula officinalis flowers on a sunny morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for four hours.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a copper alembic still with 1:4 ratio of flowers to distilled water for two hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil using coffee filters or paper towels, resulting in approximately 0.5 milliliters of pure calendula essential oil.
Store the final product in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature, allowing it to mature for several weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The oil's natural antioxidant compounds also help to reduce redness and swelling by neutralizing free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation. Additionally, the essential oil's decongestant properties help to reduce puffiness and ease discomfort, providing fast and effective relief from red, itchy, or watery eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to redness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and reduce redness in the eyes.
Barks: The barks of the Kansas coneflower contain lignans, which have antihistamine properties that help alleviate itchy and irritated eyes associated with red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated and air-dry them within 24 hours.
Steam distill dried echinacea flowers using a clevenger apparatus with 10 grams of plant material and 100 ml of water for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a paper coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 5% echinacea oil to 95% carrier oil.
Store the finished echinacea angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and keep it away from direct sunlight for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with red eyes because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's unique blend of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants helps to reduce puffiness and swelling, while also calming irritated skin. Additionally, aloe vera's natural antibacterial agents help to eliminate any underlying infections or irritants that may be contributing to redness. By applying the oil directly to the affected area, users can experience quick relief from red eyes, leaving them feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal aloe essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and redness associated with irritated or inflamed eyes.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of herbal aloe essential oil can help combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to red eyes, promoting healthy eye tissues and reducing the risk of infection.
Sotholastic: Herbal aloe essential oil has soothing and moisturizing properties that can calm dry, itchy, or irritated eyes, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Roots: Aloe roots have been traditionally used to treat eye infections and soothe irritated eyes, making them an effective addition to essential oils for red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 fresh aloe leaves from healthy plants, cutting them at the base of the leaf.
Wash the leaves thoroughly with distilled water and mild soap, rinsing well to remove dirt.
Dry the leaves for 7-10 days in a warm, well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight, allowing them to completely dry.
Steam distill the dried aloe leaves using a high-quality steam distillation apparatus according to manufacturer's instructions, collecting the essential oil as it separates from water.
Mix 20 drops of the extracted essential oil with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and store in an amber glass bottle for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with red eyes because its cooling properties help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eye tissues. The oil's menthol content also has a natural decongestant effect, which can help to relieve puffiness and reduce redness caused by allergies or sinus pressure. When applied topically, peppermint oil can provide instant relief from itchy, burning, and watery eyes, leaving them feeling calm, refreshed, and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from redness and discomfort.
C Cooling and soothing property: The menthol content in peppermint oil provides a cooling sensation when applied topically, which can help soothe itchy or irritated eyes and reduce redness.

Antioxidant property: Peppermint essential oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eye tissues, reducing inflammation and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain menthol, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease eye discomfort associated with red eyes.
Buds: The buds of peppermint plants are rich in menthone, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase them from a trusted supplier weighing about 2 ounces.
Clean and dry the harvested peppermint leaves by gently shaking off excess dirt and air-drying them for at least an hour.
Chop the dried peppermint leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area and release their oils using scissors or a sharp knife.
Place the chopped peppermint leaves in a clean glass jar with a lid and cover them with 1 pint of high-proof vodka or everclear taking about 30 minutes.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily before straining the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to obtain your mentha x piperita essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with red eyes because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically or inhaled, lavender oil can help soothe and relax the eye muscles, reducing puffiness and swelling. Its antimicrobial properties also help to reduce inflammation and combat infection-causing bacteria, which can contribute to redness and discomfort. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, English lavender essential oils can provide welcome relief from irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English Lavender essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from irritated or inflamed eyes.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of English Lavender essential oil help to prevent infections and fight off bacteria that can cause eye problems, such as pink eye (conjunctivitis).

Calming and Soothing: English Lavender essential oil's calming and soothing properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to redness in the eyes, while also providing a sense of relaxation and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a higher concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm red eyes.

Seeds: The seeds of the English lavender plant are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye irritation and inflammation associated with red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves in the morning when dew is still present for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them evenly on paper towels or cloth and leaving for 7-10 days.
Mince dried lavandula angustifolia leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for steam distillation.
Steam distill the minced lavandula angustifolia leaves with 30ml of water in a 100ml apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil, containing approximately 40% linalool, into dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains a high concentration of eucalyptol, which effectively reduces swelling and soothes irritated tissues. When applied topically to the affected area, the oil's cooling sensation helps to calm the blood vessels, reducing inflammation and redness. Additionally, its decongestant properties help to reduce excess mucus and alleviate itchiness, providing fast relief from red and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian Blue Gum Essential Oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with red eyes, such as puffiness or irritation.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Tasmanian Blue Gum Essential Oil help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to redness and irritation.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of Tasmanian Blue Gum Essential Oil help combat bacterial infections that can cause red eyes, such as conjunctivitis or blepharitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes, making them effective for relieving red eye symptoms.

Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian blue gum plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes, helping to alleviate red eye discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of eucalyptus globulus from a healthy tree at dawn when the dew is still present.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by gently brushing off excess dirt and allowing them to air dry for 24 hours.
Steam distill 100g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 500ml of water using a steam distillation apparatus for 1 hour.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water and filter it through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Store the obtained herbal eucalyptus globulus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and refrigerate at 2-3°c for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium essential oils helps with red eyes because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyes. The oil's potent antioxidants also help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, which can contribute to redness and discomfort. Additionally, wild geranium essential oils have astringent and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections that may be causing redness in the eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wild geranium essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes caused by allergies or other irritations.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of wild geranium essential oil can help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of redness and puffiness.

Sedative: Wild geranium essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety-related eye tension and discomfort, leading to less redness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help reduce swelling and soothe irritation associated with red eyes.
Barks: Containing astringent properties, the barks help constrict blood vessels and reduce redness, inflammation, and itchiness of the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh geranium maculatum leaves and stems in late morning or early afternoon when they are at their highest oil content.
Wash the harvested material gently with distilled water to remove any dirt, debris, or pesticides from the environment.
Steam-distill the washed plant material using a steam distillation apparatus for 1-2 hours to extract its essential oils.
Separate the obtained essential oil from the aqueous phase and filter it through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Store the resulting geranium maculatum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with red eyes because it contains a unique compound called sesquiterpene, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, these oils can help reduce swelling and soothe irritated conjunctiva, thus alleviating redness and discomfort. Additionally, curry plant essential oils also possess antiseptic qualities, which can prevent infection and promote healing of the eyes.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Curry plant essential oil helps reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes by combating bacterial infections and soothing irritated tissues.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in curry plant essential oil help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to redness and discomfort.

Soothing and calming effects: Curry plant essential oil has a calming and sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may exacerbate eye irritation and redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the curry plant contain antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and soothe irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks of the curry plant have astringent properties that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality helichrysum italicum flowers in the early morning, ensuring they are free from contaminants.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours, monitoring humidity levels closely.
Steam distill the dried helichrysum italicum flowers using a copper still and 500ml of distilled water for 2 hours, collecting essential oil yield.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities, resulting in a clear and fragrant oil.
Transfer the filtered essential oil into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, storing them in a cool place at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's flavonoids and antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and irritation, calming the redness and itchiness associated with dry or irritated eyes. Additionally, chamomile oil's ability to relax and calm the body can also help to reduce eye tension and stress, further reducing the appearance of red eyes. Overall, using chamomile essential oils can provide fast and effective relief from red and itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile essential oil helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with red, irritated eyes due to its ability to block the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds in chamomile essential oil neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, reducing the appearance of redness and discomfort.

Soften and calm property: Chamomile essential oil contains apigenin, an flavonoid that has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, helping to reduce redness, itchiness, and burning sensations in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile roots help soothe and calm irritated red eyes.
Flowers: The delicate petals of chamomile flowers contain soothing apigenin, which reduces eye inflammation and promotes relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning, selecting those that are free of pests and diseases.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 12 hours or air drying them for 7-10 days.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to release their essential oils.
Infuse the powdered chamomile in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:2 for 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The oil's active compounds, such as anethole and fenchol, work to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, thereby alleviating redness and discomfort. Additionally, fennel oil's decongestant properties help to narrow blood vessels, which can become dilated due to allergies or irritants, reducing redness and puffiness. By applying fennel essential oil topically or inhaling its aroma, individuals can enjoy soothing relief from red, irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel essential oil helps reduce redness and swelling associated with red eyes by reducing inflammation in the affected area.
Antioxidant properties: Fennel essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to red eye symptoms.

Vasoconstrictive properties: Fennel essential oil's ability to constrict blood vessels helps reduce blood flow to the affected area, which in turn reduces redness and alleviates discomfort associated with red eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used to create essential oils that help soothe and calm irritated eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Fennel leaves contain flavonoids, which have natural antihistamine effects, making them effective in reducing redness and itchiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare leaves in sufficient quantity for distillation.
Combine dried leaves with water in a stainless steel distillation apparatus at a ratio of 1:4.
Heat the mixture to reach a temperature of 100°c and maintain it for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distillate using a separating funnel and store it in dark glass bottles.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper or cotton cloth to remove any impurities before use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat red eyes with herbal essential oils is to mix a few drops of lavender, chamomile, and peppermint oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Gently apply the blend to your closed eyelids using a cotton swab, allowing the calming properties to soothe and reduce inflammation. Leave it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing with warm water, reducing redness and promoting relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to red eyes that are treated with herbal essential oils are dry, itchy, and irritated skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis. Certain herbs like tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile oil have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm and moisturize the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. These oils can also be used to treat minor burns and cuts, promoting healing and reducing scarring.
